title: Use manifest from container/image
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Use the manifest definitions from container/images which is what Podman does.  Best to have one central definition used by all.  

Once approved I'll add the one missing definition to containers/image and will then vendor that in and update the todo after this gets merged.

This is a WIP as I've not tested thoroughly and I don't trust myself without a double check while on cold meds.

comments.body:
Hey @TomSweeneyRedHat - so far looks good. However, it seems like the CI failed on `go fmt`.
Can you fix it?

Thanks :smile:
:clipboard: Looks like this PR is still in progress, ignoring approval
@rhatdan should we wait on `DockerV2Schema2LayerUncompressedMediaType` to be added to `images/manifest`?
Yes especially if we can get rid of the docker vendor.

@rhatdan I've gone through one last self review and a bunch of testing.  I'd like to merge this in now then will get the containers/image change in and will loop back to vendor and remove the last manifest.

I did try removing more of the Docker stuff yesterday, it go really messy, really quick fwiw.

:pushpin: Commit 90c8dc1 has been approved by `rhatdan`
